title: Non-record: TTT + QAT on Consumer GPU (val_bpb=1.5382)
body:
## val_bpb = 1.5382 (single run, standard eval + TTT)

Non-record: local consumer GPU only (1x RTX 5070 Ti, 12GB VRAM). Artifact: 11.5 MB. 300s training + 619s eval (includes TTT).

This submission was developed entirely on a laptop GPU using AI-assisted autonomous experimentation. No H100 access was used during development.

---

### Key Finding: QAT Makes Training Faster While Improving Quality

Adding fake quantization noise during training (QAT) made the model train **faster** (362ms/step vs 399ms/step) while producing a better post-quantization model. The `torch.round` + `clamp` ops in the straight-through estimator are cheaper than full-precision matmuls, so QAT is effectively free compute.

Combined with test-time training (TTT) on both attention and MLP output projections, this produced a **1.5382 BPB** result (vs 1.5540 baseline), a 1.0% improvement.

### Methodology: AI-Assisted Autonomous Experimentation

Developed using an autoresearch loop — an AI agent (Claude) autonomously modifies `train_gpt.py`, runs 5-minute experiments on a local RTX 5070 Ti, evaluates results, and iterates. 15 experiments were run with strict one-variable-at-a-time methodology.

### Architecture

9 layers, dim=512, 8 heads, 4 KV heads. MLP 2x (hidden=1024). Tied embeddings. Stock baseline architecture — no architectural changes.

### Key Modifications

- **QAT in `CastedLinear.forward()`:** Simulate int8 quantization during training via straight-through estimator. 7 lines of code.
- **TTT in `ttt_eval_val()`:** 1 SGD step (LR=1e-4) on both `attn.proj.weight` and `mlp.proj.weight` per eval batch. ~30 lines of code.

### Results

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| val_bpb (with TTT) | **1.5382** |
| val_bpb (base, no TTT) | 1.5435 |
| TTT delta | -0.005 |
| Artifact | 11,506,921 bytes |
| Steps | 779 |
| ms/step | 385 |
| Hardware | 1x RTX 5070 Ti (12GB) |

### Experiment History (15 experiments)

| # | Experiment | BPB | Status |
|---|-----------|-----|--------|
| E001 | Baseline | 1.5540 | control |
| E003 | TTT on MLP proj | 1.5265 | keep |
| E009 | TTT both attn+MLP | 1.5462 | keep |
| E011 | QAT (fake int8) | 1.5290 | keep |
| E012 | TopK 50% sparsity | 1.5566 | discard |
| E013 | MLP_MULT=3 | 1.5318 | discard |

### What Didn't Work

- **TopK MLP sparsity:** Hurt base model — not enough capacity at dim=512 for forced sparsity
- **Multi-step TTT (2 steps):** No improvement, 17min eval
- **Wider MLP (3x):** Bigger != better at ~750 training steps
- **TTT LR=3e-4:** Overshoots adaptation

### Acknowledgments

Built on the naive baseline. TTT inspired by PR #77 (samacqua). Autoresearch methodology adapted from [Karpathy's autoresearch](https://github.com/karpathy/autoresearch).
